folio.label: 73r
folio.l: That thougú he had , me bete on euäy bon
He coude wynne , my loue a geyn a noon
I trowe I loued hi~ best , for that he
Was of his loue , daungerous vn to me
We wommen han , if that I shal nat lye
In this mater , a queynt fantasie
Wayte what thyngö , we may nat ligútly haue
Ther after wyl we crye alday , and craue
¢ Forbede vs thyngö , and that desiren we
Presse on vs fast , and than wol we flee
Witú daunger outer we , al oure chaffare
Greet prees at market , maketú dere ware
And to gret chep~ , is holden at litel pris
This knowetú euäy wo~man , that is wys
¢ My ferthe husbonde , god his soule blisse
Whicú that I took , for loue & no richesse
He somtyme was , a Clerk of Oxenforde
And had left scole , and went at hom to borde
Witú my gossyp~ , dwellynge in oure tounö
God haue hire soule , hire name was Alisouö
She knew myn hert , and al my preuytee
Bet than oure parissú preest , so mot I the
To hire be wreyed I , my counseil al
For had myn husbonde , pissed on a wal
Or don a thyngö , that shuld han cost his lif
To hire , and to a nother worthy wyf
And to my Nece , whiche that I loued wel
I wold han told , his counseil euäydel
And so I dide ful often , god it woot
That mad his face , ful often red and hoot´
ffor verray shame , and blamed hi~ self for he
Had told to me , so gret a preuytee
And so byfel , that ones in a lente
So often tymes , I to my gossip~ wente
For euäe ©et , I loued to be gay
And for to walke , in Marcú ApriÂ & May
ffro hous to hous , to here sondry tales
That Iankyn Clerk , & my gossip~ Dame Ales
And I my selue , in to the feeldes went
Myn husbonde was at londou~ , al that lent
I had the betir leiser , for to pleye
And for to se , and eke for to be seye
Of lusty folk , what wyst I , where my grace
Was shapen for to be , or in what place
